subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0)
- **Game range** — The rulebook lists **1-4 players**.
- **Per-player Fear setup** — Add **4 Fear markers per player** to the Fear Pool.
- **Island scaling** — Use **1 Island Board per player**.
- **Playtime scaling note** — Rulebook notes games are **slightly shorter at 1-2 players** and **slightly longer at 4 players**.
- **No solo-specific rule block in excerpt** — The provided pages do not add separate solo-only setup exceptions.

#### Boards & Panels
- **Invader Board** — 1.
- **Modular Island Boards** — 4.
- **Spirit Panels** — 8.
- **Player Aid Cards** — 8.
- **Power Progression Cards** — 4.

#### Island Pieces
- **Dahan** — 36.
- **Cities** — 20.
- **Towns** — 32.
- **Explorers** — 40.
- **Blight tokens** — 38.

#### Spirit Markers
- **Spirit Presence** — 52 total (13 in each of 4 colors).
- **Single-Turn Effect Markers** — 12 total (3 in each of 4 colors).

#### Economy & Fear Tokens
- **Energy Markers** — 32 total.
- **1-energy markers** — 20.
- **3-energy markers** — 12.
- **Fear Markers** — 20.

#### Card Decks
- **Fear Cards** — 15.
- **Terror Dividers** — 2.
- **Blight Cards** — 2.
- **Invader Cards** — 15.
- **Major Power Cards** — 22.
- **Minor Power Cards** — 36.
- **Unique Power Cards** — 32.

#### Difficulty Modules
- **Adversary Panels** — 3.
- **Adversary Reminder Tiles** — 3.
- **Scenario Panels** — 4.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Place Invader Board** — Put it at one side of the play area.
2. **Build Fear Pool** — Add 4 Fear markers per player.
3. **Build Fear Deck** — Shuffle Fear cards, place 9, insert Terror III divider 3 cards from bottom, and Terror II divider 3 cards above that.
4. **Build Invader Deck** — Separate 15 Invader cards by stage (I/II/III, 5 each). Shuffle each stage separately, remove 1 card from each stage face-down unseen. Stack remaining 12 cards: 4 Stage I on top, 4 Stage II middle, 4 Stage III bottom.
5. **Place Blight Card** — Put a random Blight card Healthy side up without reading the back. Place 2 Blight tokens per player onto the card from the supply (2/4/6/8 for 1/2/3/4 players).
6. **Build Island** — Randomly pick one Island Board per player and arrange per player-count layout.
7. **Populate lands** — Add Invaders, Dahan, and Blight according to printed land icons.
8. **Prepare supplies** — Shuffle Minor and Major decks and place with discard space; place Cities, Towns, Explorers, Dahan, and Energy in reachable supply piles.

#### Invader Deck Construction Details
- **Stage order** — 4 Stage I on top, 4 Stage II in middle, 4 Stage III on bottom (12 total).
- **Unused cards** — 1 card removed from each stage (3 cards returned unseen to box).

#### Player-Count Setup Values
--- 1 Player ---
- **Island boards used** — 1.
- **Fear in pool** — 4.
- **Blight on card** — 2.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Island boards used** — 2.
- **Fear in pool** — 8.
- **Blight on card** — 4.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Island boards used** — 3.
- **Fear in pool** — 12.
- **Blight on card** — 6.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Island boards used** — 4.
- **Fear in pool** — 16.
- **Blight on card** — 8.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Player Setup Steps
1. **Choose color** — Take all Presence and Single-Turn Effect markers of one color.
2. **Choose Spirit** — Take one Spirit Panel and that Spirit's 4 Unique Power Cards as your starting hand.
3. **Follow panel-back setup** — Place Presence and complete any Spirit-specific extra setup.
4. **Flip panel** — Place remaining Presence on dashed circles of Presence tracks.
5. **Leave solid circles uncovered** — Keep leftmost printed values exposed as instructed.

#### First-Game Recommended Options
- **Spirit choice** — Use a low-complexity Spirit.
- **Power gains** — Use that Spirit's Power Progression card.
- **Difficulty modules** — Do not use Adversary or Scenario.
- **Blight mode** — You may skip random Blight card and use printed Invader Board blight instructions.

#### Board Reach Clarification
- **Starting board** — Each player begins on a different board.
- **Later targeting** — Presence and powers can affect any board when range/target allows.

#### Player-Count Notes
--- 1 Player ---
- **Player setup count** — 1 Spirit setup sequence.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Player setup count** — 2 Spirit setup sequences.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Player setup count** — 3 Spirit setup sequences.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Player setup count** — 4 Spirit setup sequences.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Start-of-Game Invader Action
1. **Reveal top Invader card** — Flip the top card of Invader Deck.
2. **Resolve Explore** — Invaders Explore that shown land type.
3. **Stage card placement** — Move that card face up to the Build action space.

#### Why This Matters
- **Pipeline preload** — The game starts with pressure already in the Build lane.
- **Planning baseline** — First full round decisions should account for pending Build/Ravage tempo.

- **Rulebook note** — Sessions are usually slightly longer.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Sequence
1. **Spirit Phase** — Choose Growth, gain Energy, and play/pay for Power cards.
2. **Fast Power Phase** — Resolve all Fast powers in legal order.
3. **Invader Phase** — Resolve Ravage, then Build, then Explore from the Invader Board pipeline (see **Invader Phase** subtopic for full details).
4. **Slow Power Phase** — Resolve all Slow powers played this round.
5. **Time Passes** — Clear temporary effects and check win/loss states.

#### Spirit Phase Breakdown
- **Growth** — Choose one growth option from your Spirit panel.
- **Energy** — Gain energy from uncovered tracks/effects.
- **Card play** — Choose cards up to your play limit and pay costs.

#### Timing Principle
- **Fast powers** — Immediate control and emergency responses.
- **Slow powers** — Delayed effects that shape upcoming invader turns.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Growth Actions
- **Add Presence** — Extend range and unlock stronger tracks.
- **Gain Energy** — Increase immediate spending power.
- **Reclaim Cards** — Recover used powers from discard.
- **Gain Power Card** — Add Minor or Major power to toolkit.
- **Spirit-specific options** — Resolve any extra growth text on your panel.

#### Card Play Actions
- **Play limit** — You may play up to your current card-play track value.
- **Cost payment** — Pay each played card's energy cost.
- **Legality** — Follow speed, range, target, and effect text exactly.

#### Board Interaction Actions
- **Defend** — Reduce incoming ravage damage in a land.
- **Push/Gather** — Reposition Invaders or Dahan.
- **Damage/Destroy** — Remove invader pieces when thresholds are met.
- **Fear generation** — Add fear toward Terror progression.

#### Invader Response Categories
- **Prevent** — Stop Explore/Build chains before they escalate.
- **Mitigate** — Defend key lands to prevent blight.
- **Punish** — Clear invaders after they cluster.

#### Loss-Check Triggers
- **Supply exhaustion** — If required invader pieces cannot be placed, defeat may trigger.
- **Blight exhaustion** — If blight-loss condition is met, defeat triggers.
- **Spirit destruction** — If a spirit is destroyed completely, defeat triggers.

subtopics.title: Invader Phase
subtopics.content:
#### Invader Phase Pipeline
The Invader Phase resolves three steps in strict order using the cards on the Invader Board.

##### Ravage
- Invaders in lands matching the **Ravage card** deal damage: **1** per Explorer, **2** per Town, **3** per City.
- If total damage to a land is **2** or more, add **1** Blight to that land (flip from Blight card).
- Dahan in the land fight back — each Dahan deals **2** damage to Invaders (Dahan have **2** health each).

##### Build
- In lands matching the **Build card**, Invaders add **1** Town if no Town or City is present, or upgrade **1** Town to a City if a Town is already present.

##### Explore
- Add **1** Explorer to each land matching the **Explore card** that is adjacent to a Town, City, or Ocean.

#### Blight Cascade
- If Blight is added to a land that **already has Blight**, cascade **1** additional Blight to an adjacent land.
- Cascades can chain — if the adjacent land also already has Blight, cascade again.

#### Fear Generation
- Destroying a **Town** generates **1** Fear.
- Destroying a **City** generates **2** Fear.
- When enough Fear accumulates, a Fear card is earned and resolved during the next Invader Phase.

#### Piece Health
- **Explorers** — **1** health.
- **Towns** — **2** health.
- **Cities** — **3** health.
- **Dahan** — **2** health.

#### Terror Level Victory Conditions
- **Terror Level I** — Win by destroying **all** Invaders (no Explorers, Towns, or Cities remain on the island).
- **Terror Level II** — Win by destroying all Towns and Cities (Explorers may remain).
- **Terror Level III** — Win by destroying all Cities (Towns and Explorers may remain).
- **Terror Level Victory** (all Fear cards earned) — Win immediately.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Asymmetry System
- **Spirit identity** — Each Spirit has distinct growth, tracks, and powers.
- **Role coverage** — Teams should coordinate defense, control, fear, and damage roles.

#### Fear & Terror System
- **Fear deck layout** — 9 active Fear cards split into 3 groups by Terror dividers.
- **Progress effect** — Higher Terror levels reduce required invader elimination to win. See **Invader Phase — Terror Level Victory Conditions** for exact thresholds.
- **Fear generation** — Destroying a Town generates **1** Fear; destroying a City generates **2** Fear.

#### Blight System
- **Healthy start** — Blight card begins on Healthy side unless first-game variant skips it.
- **Pressure clock** — Blight growth is a loss clock, not just local damage.
- **Cascade rule** — Blight added to a land that already has Blight cascades **1** additional Blight to an adjacent land.

#### Invader AI Pipeline
- **Predictability** — Invaders follow a card-driven Ravage → Build → Explore sequence with visible upcoming pressure.
- **Planning value** — Teams can plan one or more steps ahead by reading the pipeline.
- **Damage values** — Explorers deal **1**, Towns deal **2**, Cities deal **3** during Ravage.

#### First-Game Ruleset (from excerpt)
- **Low-complexity spirits** — Recommended.
- **Power progression cards** — Recommended.
- **No adversary/scenario** — Recommended.
- **Optional no-blight-card mode** — Allowed.

#### Player-Count Scaling
--- 1-2 Players ---
- **Rulebook pace note** — Games are usually 1-2 turns shorter on average.
--- 4 Players ---
- **Rulebook pace note** — Games are usually 1-2 turns longer on average.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Conditions
- **Co-op outcome** — The game ends as a team win or team loss.
- **No VP winner race** — Standard outcome is not player-vs-player points.

#### Victory Condition
- **Terror Level I** — Win by destroying **all** Invaders (no Explorers, Towns, or Cities remain).
- **Terror Level II** — Win by destroying all Towns and Cities (Explorers may remain).
- **Terror Level III** — Win by destroying all Cities (Towns and Explorers may remain).
- **Terror Level Victory** (all Fear cards earned) — Win immediately.
- **Fear impact** — Fear progression advances Terror level, lowering what must be eliminated.

#### Defeat Conditions (from provided text)
- **Blight overrun** — If island is overrun by blight, you lose.
- **Spirit destroyed** — If any Spirit is destroyed completely, you lose.
- **Too slow/entrenched invaders** — If you fail to drive invaders off before entrenchment, you lose.
- **Supply-based defeat** — If required placement is impossible due to supply exhaustion, defeat can trigger.

#### Check Timing
- **Immediate checks** — Check defeat when triggering state occurs.
- **Phase checks** — Re-check outcomes at end-of-round timing in sequence flow.

#### Scoring Note
- **Rulebook TOC** — A Scoring section exists for post-game evaluation.
- **Core result** — Standard game result is still binary win/loss.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early-Round Priorities
- **Read pipeline first** — Identify current Ravage, next Build, and new Explore pressure before choosing growth.
- **Triaging** — Stop chains where one intervention prevents multiple future problems.
- **Growth discipline** — Build Presence economy early so midgame turns do not stall.
- **Fast-power targeting** — Spend Fast effects on lands where blight or city growth risk is highest.
- **Role coverage** — Assign at least one defense lane and one control lane across spirits.
- **Dahan value** — Preserve Dahan clusters when possible for efficient counterattacks.

#### Player-Count Focus
--- 1 Player ---
- **Coverage rule** — On your 1 board, plan to cover at least 2 of 3 jobs each round (defense, control, scaling), then use growth to patch the missing lane next turn.
--- 2 Players ---
- **Pair planning** — Split responsibilities into 2 lanes each round (for example: defense + control) to avoid overlap.
--- 3 Players ---
- **Role split** — Use 1 spirit for stabilization, 1 for control, and 1 for conversion (fear/damage).
--- 4 Players ---
- **Coordination load** — Agree on top 4 priority lands first, then assign exact lands quickly to reduce analysis drag.

#### First-Game Practicality
- **Use tutorial options** — Low-complexity spirits + progression cards improve early decision quality.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Mistake 1 — Solving every land
- **Problem** — Early turns cannot cover every invader action.
- **Fix** — Prioritize highest-consequence lands first.

#### Mistake 2 — Skipping growth
- **Problem** — Low tracks cap your energy/card-play tempo.
- **Fix** — Commit to at least one meaningful growth action early.

#### Mistake 3 — Speed mismatch
- **Problem** — Strong Slow cards can be one phase too late.
- **Fix** — Match card speed to the exact threat window.

#### Mistake 4 — Fear neglect
- **Problem** — Ignoring fear can delay easier Terror-level win conditions.
- **Fix** — Include fear generation in core plan, not as bonus text.

#### Mistake 5 — Uncoordinated planning
- **Problem** — Double-covering one land leaves another critical land exposed.
- **Fix** — Assign land ownership during planning: who handles which threat.

#### Mistake 6 — Blight denial
- **Problem** — "One more blight" thinking can become sudden loss.
- **Fix** — Track blight state every turn and pivot earlier.

#### Mistake 7 — Early major overreach
- **Problem** — Expensive majors can clog turns before economy matures.
- **Fix** — Stabilize energy/card-play engine before major-heavy lines.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1 — Prevent vs recover
- **Prevent line** — Stop Build/Explore chains before they produce larger threats.
- **Recover line** — Fix immediate ravage damage when cascade risk is critical.

#### Decision 2 — Growth now vs cover now
- **Growth now** — Improves future turns and long-run stability.
- **Cover now** — Buys survival when a defeat trigger is near.

#### Decision 3 — Fear race vs board clear
- **Fear race** — Push Terror quickly when fear engine is strong.
- **Board clear** — Prioritize piece removal when tactical control is strong.

#### Decision 4 — Presence placement lane
- **Local placement** — Solves immediate short-range tactical needs.
- **Network placement** — Increases future multi-board flexibility.

#### Decision 5 — Planning depth vs tempo
- **Deep planning** — Higher efficiency but slower rounds.
- **Tempo planning** — Faster rounds with acceptable imperfect optimization.

#### Decision 6 — Difficulty module timing
- **Add later** — Introduce Adversary/Scenario after core timing skills are reliable.
- **Avoid stack too early** — Too many modules at once can hide baseline mistakes.

metadata.notes: Written from OCR-readable Spirit Island rulebook PDF pages (components, setup, sequence framing, victory/defeat framing). Uses exact counts visible in provided pages.
